audioModeReplaceDesc: Replace the original audio with the dub track; output keeps only the dub
audioModeMixDesc: Duck the original audio while the dub speaks (keeps background sound), mixed into one track
audioModeAddTrackDesc: Keep both original and dub as switchable audio tracks; output is an MKV container
audioModeMkvHint: Dual-track mode outputs an MKV container; the output extension has been adjusted automatically.
styleModeOutlineDesc: Text with outline and shadow, works for most videos
styleModeBoxDesc: Text on a color block, clearer over busy footage
outlineWidthHint: Thickness of the text outline; 0 means no outline. An outline greatly improves readability when subtitles blend into the footage
shadowHint: Offset of the drop shadow at the lower right of the text; 0 means no shadow
backgroundOpacityHint: Opacity of the background block: 100% fully covers the footage, lower values let it show through
boxPaddingHint: Space between the text and the edge of the background box; larger values make the block roomier
marginsHint: Distance between subtitles and the frame edges; affects position and line-wrap width
selectVideoToPreview: Please select a video file to preview
outputModeHardcodeDesc: Subtitles rendered into the picture, works in every player (slower)
outputModeSoftmuxDesc: Finishes in seconds, lossless quality, toggleable in players
videoQualityHint: Burn-in re-encodes the video. Original stays closest to the source with the largest file; Standard is smaller with slightly lower quality. Soft-mux is lossless and unaffected by this option.
encoderModeHardwareDesc: Hardware-accelerated encoding via {{encoder}} — much faster; at the same quality the output may be 30%–100% larger than CPU encoding
encoderModeUnavailable: No usable hardware encoder detected; CPU encoding is used
hwAccelSizeHint: Hardware acceleration greatly speeds up merging, but at the same quality the output may be 30%–100% larger than CPU encoding. Prefer CPU if file size matters.
hwFallbackNotice: Hardware encoding failed; automatically retried with CPU encoding
styleOnlyForHardcode: Soft subtitle appearance is controlled by the player. Style settings below only apply to burn-in mode
outputPathRequiredHint: Video and subtitle are selected — choose an output path before merging
myPresetsEmpty: Tweak the style, then hit "Save as my style" — saved styles are also available in the new-task wizard
savePresetDialogDesc: Save the current style as a reusable preset, available in the compose workbench and the new-task wizard.
savePresetNamePlaceholder: Style name, e.g. Big yellow captions
presetSaveFailed: Failed to save the style, please retry

emptyGuide.step1Desc: Choose files in the bar above — the preview appears instantly
emptyGuide.step2Desc: Pick a preset or fine-tune font, position and effects with live rendering
emptyGuide.step3Desc: Choose burn-in or soft mux, confirm the output path, and go

pageDesc: Burn subtitles into the video, or mux them as a soft subtitle track
